* Initial plan

Add POST /api/spaces/:spaceId/find-similar REST endpoint, find_similar
MCP tool, and findSimilar() core function. Uses stored embedding vectors

self-match exclusion.

Add Copilot Readiness Checker — free tool #8

Interactive 30-question assessment scoring M365 Copilot readiness
across 7 pillars (licensing, identity, data governance, security,
network, change management, monitoring). Features animated score ring,
per-pillar breakdown bars, actionable recommendations with Learn docs
links, shareable URLs, print-friendly view, keyboard navigation.
100% client-side JS, zero API calls.

- Blue #3B82F6 accent, readiness.css + readiness.js
- Added to nav dropdown, free-tools page, baseof body class
- Cross-links to Licensing Simplifier, M365 Roadmap, Service Health

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add blob API round-trip, fix cache logging, add version endpoint

- POST/GET/DELETE /api/blobs/* for app-level blob access
- GET /api/blobs/roundtrip for automated write/read/verify/delete test
- GET /api/version for environment isolation proof
- Add cache set/get logging to debug cache behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: production hardening — rate limiting, structured logging, error handlers, TLS

- Rate limiting: slowapi on all auth endpoints (10/min login, 30/min /me, 5/min account delete)
- Structured logging: JSON formatter via python-json-logger, log auth events, room lifecycle, errors
- Global error handler: catch-all returns generic 500, logs real traceback server-side
- Validation error handler: sanitized 422 responses without pydantic internals
- Security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, HSTS
- CORS restricted: explicit methods (GET/POST/DELETE/OPTIONS) and headers instead of wildcards
- TLS infrastructure: self-signed cert generator, nginx-ssl.conf, docker-compose.prod.yml, livekit-prod.yaml
- Debug mode: docs/redoc hidden when DEBUG=false, HSTS only in production
- 12 new tests covering all hardening features (50 total backend tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Configures Gluetun VPN for media apps (#3800)

* Configures VPN firewall for cluster subnets

Adds `FIREWALL_OUTBOUND_SUBNETS` to Deluge and qBittorrent deployments. This allows these applications, which route traffic through a Gluetun VPN, to reach internal cluster services on the `10.42.0.0/16` and `10.43.0.0/16` subnets, preventing necessary internal traffic from being blocked.

* Adds Gluetun firewall input ports

Configures `FIREWALL_INPUT_PORTS` for Deluge and qBittorrent in their StatefulSets. This ensures required inbound ports are opened through the Gluetun sidecar, enabling proper torrent client functionality and web UI access.

* Ensures VPN routing table cleanup on container start

Adds a post-start lifecycle hook to Deluge and qBittorrent containers to remove potentially stale IP routing rules for table 51820. This prevents network routing conflicts and ensures proper VPN functionality, aligning with recommendations for Gluetun deployments in Kubernetes.

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Erik Rasmussen <erik.rasmussen@unmango.dev>

Co-authored-by: fepegar <12688084+fepegar@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: fix DebugHost /engine startup from 6s to sub-1ms (#77)

* perf: fix DebugHost /engine startup from 6s to sub-1ms (#76)

Root cause: DebugHost defaulted to http://localhost:50051 while the Rust
engine binds 127.0.0.1:50051. On Windows, localhost resolves to ::1 first,
adding ~2s per gRPC channel. The /engine page created 3 channels
sequentially, paying the penalty 3x.

Changes:

Layer 1 - Fix default endpoint:
- Change DefaultEngineEndpoint from localhost to 127.0.0.1 in
  DebugWorkbenchOptions, appsettings.json, appsettings.Development.json,
  Engine.razor guidance text, and CliArgumentParser.

Layer 2 - Reuse GrpcChannel per endpoint:
- Convert GrpcClientFactory to cache a single GrpcChannel per endpoint
  lifetime with thread-safe invalidation on endpoint change.
- Configure SocketsHttpHandler with EnableMultipleHttp2Connections,
  KeepAlivePingDelay (60s), PooledConnectionIdleTimeout (5m).
- Remove channel-per-operation dispose pattern from all 5 workbench
  services (SetupWorkbenchService, StationProfileWorkbenchService,
  RuntimeConfigWorkbenchService, LookupWorkbenchService,
  StorageWorkbenchService).

Layer 3 - Parallel startup RPCs:
- Change Engine.razor OnInitializedAsync from sequential awaits to
  Task.WhenAll for the 3 independent refresh calls.

Layer 5 - Cache ToolchainLocator results:
- Cache PATH scan results in singleton on first call.
- Fix GetToolAvailability calling each Find method twice per tool.

Benchmarks - Add regression harness:
- Add BenchmarkDotNet project with 9 scenarios across localhost/127.0.0.1.
- New scenarios: warm channel reuse, tuned SocketsHttpHandler, cold
  channel isolation, parallel on warm channel.

BenchmarkDotNet results (warm channel, parallel RPCs):
  Before: 6,079 ms (3 channels, sequential, localhost)
  After:    0.88 ms (warm channel, parallel, either host)
  Improvement: 6,907x

Named pipes evaluated and deferred: warm channel parallel is already
sub-millisecond on TCP. Named pipes would add dual-transport complexity
for ~0.5ms theoretical gain.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use double-checked locking in GrpcClientFactory

Replace lock-on-every-call with volatile CachedChannel record and
double-checked locking. The lock is now only taken on the cold path
(first call or endpoint change), avoiding contention on the hot path.

Addresses review feedback from Treit on PR #77.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: High performance Erlang MTProto proxy that powers https://t.me/socksy_bot

commit.message:
Fix merge conflict in WaitlistForm; install @vercel/analytics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add split-mode: front/back nodes via Erlang distribution

Adds `node_role = front | back | both` (default `both` — no behaviour change).
In split mode the domestic front node runs Ranch listeners and session/policy
processes; the foreign back node runs mtp_config, DC pools, and downstream
connections.

The key implementation insight: Erlang's transparent distributed process
addressing means `gen_server:call({mtp_dc_pool_1, BackNode}, ...)` works
identically to a local call — zero changes to the hot-path message protocol.
Process monitors fire on node disconnection, so back-node restarts propagate
cleanly to front-node handlers without any watcher process.

Changes:
- mtproto_proxy_sup: role-parameterised children/1
- mtproto_proxy_app: role-gated start, config_changed, port management
- mtp_config: backend_node/0, remote-aware get_downstream_pool/1 and
  get_default_dc/0 using erpc:call; get_downstream_safe/2 dispatches
  remotely in front mode
- mtp_metric: passive_metrics/0 skips unavailable gauges per role
- split_dc_SUITE: two CT cases (echo, migration) on OTP peer nodes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(dashboard): Sprint 6 — 10 dashboard improvements with QA fixes

Changes:
- Move Active/Used badge to period banner (smaller, status-aware)
- Shrink benefit card height (min-h 200→160, reduced padding)
- Move delete to edit modal; add claimedAt date picker + Mark Unused
- Annual benefits show 'Mark Used' instead of 'Mark Jan Used'
- Progress ring always shows %, yearly progress via calculateYearlyUsage()
- Fix spacing gap after cards (py-8→py-6)
- Total benefits sums across ALL cards with deduplication
- Period filters reordered: All Time → Year → Half-year → Quarter → Month
- 13 benefit category icons (insurance, entertainment, streaming, etc.)

QA fixes (3 rounds):
- Fix data-loss: merge partial API responses instead of full replace
- Fix status filter: isUsed checked before apiStatus for correct filtering
- Fix stale closures: all setBenefits calls use functional updater pattern
- Add server-side claimedAt validation in PATCH route
- Fix null clearing for userDeclaredValue, claimedAt, expirationDate
- Fix userDeclaredValue=0 display (use != null, not truthiness)
- Fix expiration date validation (compare date-only, not datetime)
- Fix unit mismatch in new dashboard (cents→dollars conversion)
- Remove dead code: unused onDelete prop, _usage param, aria-disabled
- Remove role=status from period banner (prevents excessive SR announcements)
- Fix date picker helper text for partial period bounds

commit.message:
fix: send track_id as query param, not HTTP header (#13)

/auth/session/ expects track_id as a URL query parameter (?track_id=...),
not as an HTTP header. With a header the endpoint ignores the track and
returns generic cookies without auth context — Quasar IoT returns 401.

Closes #13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(networks): propagate data-driven encoder stats to output layer

Derive layer-specific train_data for whitened, class_contrast, and
local_pca encoders as activities move through a multi-layer NEFNetwork,
so hidden and output layers can initialize data-driven encoders in their
own activity space instead of only the first hidden layer receiving
encoder_kwargs.

Also reuse those encoder kwargs for hybrid incremental initialization so
multi-layer data-driven configurations do not fail when that path is
used.

Add regression tests covering greedy multi-layer construction with all
three data-driven encoder strategies.

Motivation: the defaults_tp Colab suite failed on Net greedy whitened
mnist because the output layer still used encoder_strategy=whitened
without receiving train_data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Wave 2 — 7 parallel agents (Sonnet 4.6 + GPT-5.4):

Context Eviction (#190-197):

- Atomic MessageGroups, CompactionTriggers (AND/OR composition)

Cognitive Health (#198-203, #206, #214):

Skill Evolution (#207-212, #215):
- SkillEvolver (session-driven skill creation)

Plugins + Teams (#52-54, #123-127, #154, #213):

fix(session): pass track_id as query param, not header

The Passport session endpoint /auth/session/ expects track_id as a
query parameter. The ya-passport-auth library (and our previous
workaround) sent it as an HTTP header, which the endpoint ignores —
the redirect chain completes but no session cookies are set.

Match AlexxIT's original approach: params={'track_id': ...} with
allow_redirects=False, checking for 302 status. Also use passport_host
from the auth bundle response.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
bug: Portfolio — Verfügbar > Gesamt-Budget (falsches Alpaca API Feld buying_power statt cash) (#71)

* fix: Portfolio Verfügbar zeigt cash statt buying_power (Fixes #70)

buying_power bei Alpaca Paper = 2× Cash (Margin-basiert) und ist daher
größer als das Gesamt-Budget equity. Korrekt ist cash als verfügbarer
Betrag. Damit gilt: Investiert = equity - cash (≈ PLTR Market Value)
und Verfügbar ≤ Budget (mathematisch korrekt).

Co-authored-by: Mario Schmelzer <oiramsch@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: normalize Alpaca buying_power to cash in AlpacaConnector.get_balance()

commit.message:
Fix server-side safety, performance, and code quality issues

Critical:
- Fix buffer overflow: dynamic buffer sizing for symbols (was fixed 64B)
- Handle WebSocket partial messages: check EndOfMessage, reject fragments

Performance:
- Use ArrayPool<byte> for hot-path event serialization (orders, trades, info)
- Avoid re-freezing FrozenDictionary on new instruments during hot path
- Write UTF8 directly to dest span (no intermediate byte[] allocation)
- Cap incremental queue at 500K to bound memory during slow recovery

Architecture:
- WebSocketHost: IDisposable → IAsyncDisposable (no sync-over-async)
- Configure WebSocket KeepAliveInterval (30s)
- Extract TryValidateAndResolve to deduplicate HandleSubscribe/HandleGet
- Extract ForwardTradeEvent to deduplicate OnTrade/OnForwardTrade
- Add InstrumentInfo.Reset() to replace fragile manual field clearing
- Replace magic template IDs (12, 30) with generated constants

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(chart): persist period selection and compact period picker UI (#595)

* feat(chart): persist period selection and move toggles inline with section header

- Add lastChartPeriod field to extension; defaults to 'day'
- Handle 'setPeriodPreference' message in showChart() to store chosen period
- Pass initialPeriod in getChartHtml() so chart reopens on the last-used period
- In bootstrap(), set currentPeriod from initialData.initialPeriod before render
- In switchPeriod(), post setPeriodPreference message to extension
- Move period toggle buttons from separate row into chart section header (right-aligned)
- Period pills are smaller (11px font, 4px/9px padding) and sit alongside the heading
- Saves one full row of vertical space; compact pills save screen real estate
- Add .chart-section-header flex layout and .period-controls compact overrides

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(cache): raise session file cache limit from 1000 to 3000 entries

With ~1100 session files, the old 1000-entry limit caused constant evictions
on every analysis cycle, keeping cache hit rates around 50-60%.

3000 entries gives comfortable headroom (3x current file count). Memory cost
is ~1-2 MB which is well within VS Code extension and globalState limits.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(chart): group minor models into 'Other' and exclude Unknown from repo view

By Model chart:
- Rank all models by total tokens for the active period
- Show top 5 models individually (largest usage first)
- Collapse remaining models into a single 'Other models' dataset (gray)
- Eliminates the wall of 20+ legend entries visible in the screenshot

By Repository chart:
- Exclude 'Unknown' entries from repository datasets and summary cards
- 'Unknown' represents sessions with no repo context (empty window chats,
  global CLI sessions, etc.) which add noise to a per-repo breakdown
- Same filter applied to repositoryTotalsMap used for the detail panel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(repo-detection): extract repo from CLI tool args and handle git worktrees

Two bugs that caused CLI sessions in worktrees to show as 'Unknown' in
the By Repository chart:

1. CLI JSONL path extraction was dead code:
   allContentReferences was defined but never populated for non-delta
   (Copilot CLI) JSONL files. The loop only read rename_session calls.
   Now also reads tool.execution_start argument values that look like
   file paths and pushes them into allContentReferences, which is then
   fed to extractRepositoryFromContentReferences as before.

2. Git worktree detection missing in workspaceHelpers.ts:
   extractRepositoryFromContentReferences only checked for .git/config
   (standard git repo). Worktrees have .git as a FILE containing
   'gitdir: <path>/.git/worktrees/<name>'. This file was unreadable as
   a directory, silently caught, and the walk continued upward past the
   repo root without finding the remote URL.
   Now also reads .git as a file, follows the gitdir pointer, resolves
   the main .git dir (2 levels up from the worktree-specific dir), and
   reads the remote URL from its config.

Bump CACHE_VERSION 37 → 38 to invalidate stale 'no repo' cache entries
so all existing CLI sessions are re-scanned on next reload.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: open Usage Analysis panel immediately, load stats in background

Previously showUsageAnalysis() awaited calculateUsageAnalysisStats() before
creating the panel, causing a ~20s blank delay when the cache was cold
(e.g. while the chart was computing its 365-day background load).

Apply the same two-phase pattern used by the chart view:
- Create the webview panel immediately
- If lastUsageAnalysisStats is cached, render it instantly
- Otherwise show a loading spinner; fire calculateUsageAnalysisStats() in the
  background and push 'updateStats' to the webview when it completes
- getUsageAnalysisHtml() now accepts UsageAnalysisStats | null
- bootstrap() shows a loading message instead of 'No data available.' when
  null, since the updateStats message handler already calls renderLayout()

commit.message:
fix: AvailableYears combo empty due to local Publish().RefCount() (#12)

The local Publish().RefCount() on inputEntries/outputEntries in both
FiscalYearViewModel and SimulationViewModel caused the second OAPH
subscriber (availableYears) to miss the initial DynamicData replay.

Publish() uses a plain Subject which does not replay. The first OAPH
(entries) subscribes and triggers the RefCount connection, consuming
the synchronous initial emission. The second OAPH (availableYears)
subscribes after and gets nothing from the Subject.

Fix: remove Publish().RefCount() so each OAPH independently subscribes
to EntriesCollection and receives its own DynamicData replay.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Overhaul dungeon generation: full grid-based BSP with tile renderer

Replace lazy tree generation (generateExit) with full pre-generation using
Prim's MST room placement and L-shaped corridor carving on a 50x32 tile grid.

Changes:
- Add TILE constant (WALL/FLOOR/CORRIDOR/DOOR)
- Replace newDungeon() with full BSP grid generator (up to 12 rooms, MST corridors)
- Remove generateExit() entirely
- Replace depth() to return dungeon.floor
- Update spawnMonster() signature (no arg needed, uses dungeon.floor internally)
- Replace MapRender.draw() with tile-based grid renderer (6px tiles, fog of war)
- Update Game.move() to use room.connections instead of node.exits
- Update buildActions() movement to iterate connections (shows '(?)' for unexplored)
- Replace hardcoded 'Flee to Town' button with entrance-only 'Exit to Town'
- Remove flee/parentId logic; use connections to backtrack instead
- Update updateFloorInfo() to show visited/total room count
- Update canvas to 320x320, refresh map legend colors
- Remove orphaned .btn-flee-town CSS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
